description: Synthetic QA cases driving the simulated LLM in benchmarks.hallucination_rate. Each query begins with a bracketed tag that the simulator inspects to decide that attempt's quality, exercising every routing branch of the graph deterministically.

tags.[EASY]: Confident, well-cited claim on the first attempt.
tags.[LOW_CONF]: Always returns a low-confidence claim — FactCheckGate rejects.
tags.[BAD_SOURCE]: High confidence but invalid (http://) source — FactCheckGate rejects.
tags.[CRITIC_FAIL]: High confidence with valid source but Critic flags a contradiction.
tags.[RECOVERS]: Low confidence on attempt 1, confident on attempt 2 — success after retry.

cases.query: [EASY] What is the capital of France?
[EASY] What color is the sky on a clear day?
[LOW_CONF] What is the speed of dark?
[LOW_CONF] What did Napoleon think on his last Tuesday?
[BAD_SOURCE] Cite the boiling point of water.
[CRITIC_FAIL] Give a plausibly-cited but contradictory claim.
[RECOVERS] Low confidence first, confident on retry.
